title: dactylorhiza maculata subsp. transsilvanica
intro: Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. transsilvanica, also known as the spotted orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family and is native to Eastern Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. transsilvanica include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to support respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
